mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate rate hikes, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with CPIAUCSL momentum (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS exceeds its 6-month moving average by more than 2%, consumer spending momentum accelerates, boosting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when RSAFS growth falls below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in PAYEMS and decreases in UNRATE indicate a tightening labor market that raises wage inflation expectations, prompting equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k and UNRATE drops below 3.5%, or interaction term IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO above its 12-month trend signals resolving supply chain bottlenecks, which improves profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO crosses above trend during periods of CPIAUCSL > 8%, or rolling IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising FEDFUNDS and elevated CPIAUCSL creates a stagflationary pricing environment that compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS rises below 0.5% per month, or 20-day returns remain positive in >55% of high-inflation tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher GDPC1 growth rates directly increase corporate revenue forecasts, which translates into sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions propagate.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth, or Rank IC remains below 0.015 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises above 4.0% in a single month, it signals an abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.0%, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during Fed pause periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST permits indicate strengthening residential construction activity, which boosts industrial demand and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days during low-rate environments.
failure_condition: 20-day returns turn negative when HOUST rises alongside FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 48% in high-rate regimes.
